This prophecy is against Edom. Their destruction appears to have been typical, like their father Esau's rejection; and to refer to the destruction of the enemies of the gospel church. See the prediction of the success of that war: Edom will be plundered and brought down. All the enemies of God’s church will be disappointed in what they rely on. God can easily lay low those who magnify and exalt themselves, and He will do it. Carnal security prepares people for ruin, and makes the ruin worse when it arrives. Treasures on earth cannot be stored so safely that thieves may not break in and steal; therefore, it is our wisdom to store up treasures in heaven. Those who make flesh their trust arm themselves against themselves. The God of our covenant will never deceive us, but if we trust people with whom we join ourselves, it may prove a wound and dishonor to us. God will justly deny understanding to those who refuse to use their understandings to avoid sin. All violence, all unrighteousness, is sin; but it makes the violence far worse when done against any of God’s people. Their cruel treatment of Judah and Jerusalem is charged upon them. When we reflect on ourselves, it is good to consider what we should have done; to compare our actions with Scripture's rule. Sin, viewed in this way, will appear exceedingly sinful. Those who are idle observers of their neighbors’ troubles when they could be active helpers have much to answer for. People make themselves poor if they think to enrich themselves by ruining God’s people; and those deceive themselves who call all that they can grasp their own on a day of calamity. Though judgment begins at the house of God, it will not end there. Let sorrowful believers and arrogant oppressors know that the troubles of the righteous will soon end, but the troubles of the wicked will be eternal.

There should be deliverance and holiness in Jerusalem, and the people of Jacob would again occupy their possessions. Much of this prophecy was fulfilled when the Jews returned to their own land. But the salvation and holiness of the gospel, its spread, and the conversion of the Gentiles also seem to be intended, especially the restoration of Israel, the destruction of antichrist, and the prosperous state of the church, which all the prophets confirm. When Christ comes, and not before, the kingdom will belong to the Lord in the fullest sense. Just as those who exalt themselves against the Lord will not prosper, and all will be brought down, so those who wait on the Lord and put their trust in Him will never be dismayed. Blessed be the Divine Savior and Judge on Mount Zion! His word will be a fragrance of life to many, while it judges and condemns stubborn unbelievers.
